questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If she studies harder, ___"
Complete the sentence: "We'll miss the train ___"
Complete the sentence: "If it rains tomorrow, ___"
Complete the sentence: "He won't get the job ___"
Complete the sentence: "If you don't eat breakfast, ___"
If you _____ (not study), you will fail the test.
She _____ (be) happy if you call her tonight.
If they _____ (miss) the bus, they will take a taxi.
We won't go to the beach if it _____ (rain).
If he _____ (work) harder, he will get a promotion.
Find the mistake: "If it will rain, we will cancel the picnic."
questions.answer: We don't use 'will' in the if-clause. Correct: 'If it rains,'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She will passes the exam if she studies."
questions.answer: After 'will', use the base form: 'will pass'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "If they don't hurry, they will to miss the bus."
Find the mistake: "We will go to park if it is sunny tomorrow."
Find the mistake: "If he will study tonight, he'll do well in the exam."
questions.answer: No 'will' in the if-clause. Correct: 'If he studies tonight,'
If you leave early, you'll catch the train.
If you don't study, you will fail the exam.
If it rains, we won't arrive on time.
She will be angry if he's late.
If she arrives late, he will be angry.
